hypothesis: Already-selected risk_on signals with no other sizing modifier and regime_exit_score >= 0.20 may deserve a larger non-stacking risk budget than the generic 1.25x plain risk_on lift.
why_not_llm_soft_ranking: LLM soft-ranking remains production-sample limited; this experiment used deterministic fields already present in both production and backtest sizing. The data limitation did not block this capital-allocation alpha test.

market_regime_summary.mid_weak: rotation-heavy bull after accepted allocation stack
market_regime_summary.old_thin: mixed-to-weak older tape with the weakest win rate

gate4_basis: Rejected and rolled back. The temporary shared policy patch produced no metric movement in any fixed window, so it failed all Gate 4 improvement criteria.
mechanism_insight: The remaining high-score plain risk_on inventory is not an obvious materiality unlock under current cap/heat/position constraints. A simple 1.5x high-score lift changed the intended multiplier path but released no executed alpha.

llm_metrics.blocker_relation: LLM soft-ranking remains data-limited, so this deterministic sizing alpha was tested instead.

rejection_reason: No EV, PnL, Sharpe, drawdown, trade count, win rate, or survival-rate change in any of the three canonical windows.
do_not_repeat_without_new_evidence: Nearby high-score plain risk_on multiplier tuning
Further broad risk_on score-bucket sizing unless a cap/heat attribution proves the change can execute
next_retry_requires: Position-level evidence that high-score plain risk_on signals are not cap/heat constrained
Forward sample showing high-score plain risk_on trades have distinct tail-adjusted expectancy
An orthogonal event/state discriminator rather than another score-bucket multiplier
